name: Lissandra
playstyleSummary: Lissandra is a control mage who fights at close-to-medium range, chaining crowd control together into burst damage before repositioning or escaping with her teleport dash. Her ultimate can either lock down an enemy with a stun or turn her briefly invulnerable to survive a burst of damage.
strengths: Strong crowd control chain (slow into root into stun) that can lock a target down alone
Glacial Path gives great mobility to reposition or escape
Her ultimate can save her life by making her untargetable and invulnerable
Good at controlling fights from the front line of her own team
weaknesses: Shorter ability ranges than most mages, forcing riskier positioning
Vulnerable if her Glacial Path is on cooldown or she's caught before using it
Relies on landing multiple abilities in sequence for full impact
Ring of Frost has a long cooldown early, leaving her without her root

abilities.name: Iceborn Subjugation
abilities.howToUseIt: When an enemy champion dies near you, they leave behind a Frozen Thrall - stay nearby to benefit from the slow and burst of damage when it shatters.

abilities.name: Ice Shard
abilities.howToUseIt: Line this shard up through multiple enemies since it passes through and damages everyone it hits, not just the first target.

abilities.name: Ring of Frost
abilities.howToUseIt: Use this to root enemies who get too close - since it has a long cooldown early, save it for a real engage or to protect yourself rather than to poke.

abilities.name: Glacial Path
abilities.howToUseIt: Send the claw out and reactivate it to blink to that location - use it to close distance onto a target or to escape when in danger.

abilities.name: Frozen Tomb
abilities.howToUseIt: Cast it on a key enemy to stun them during a fight, or cast it on yourself when about to die to become invulnerable and heal back up.

earlyGamePlan: In lane, poke with Ice Shard when you can hit multiple targets, and hold Ring of Frost until the enemy commits to a trade or dive since it recharges slowly at low ranks. Keep Glacial Path ready as your main escape tool rather than using it aggressively before level 6.
quickTips: Approach from behind your minion wave so Ice Shard's slow catches a minion first, not you
Immobilize Lissandra before she reactivates Glacial Path to stop her escape
Self-cast your ultimate (hotkey plus self-cast key) to instantly wrap yourself in ice when low
Fake out enemies by casting Glacial Path one direction, then walking the other way
Watch for Ring of Frost's cooldown and engage while it's down
Stay at max range since Lissandra's abilities are shorter than most mages'
whatToExpect: Lissandra will try to land Ice Shard and Ring of Frost to slow and root you before finishing with Frozen Tomb for a stun, so try not to get caught alone. If she uses Glacial Path to dash in, immobilizing her immediately can prevent her from teleporting away - and remember she can also cast her ultimate on herself to become invulnerable if your team focuses her.

glossary.definition: Chipping away at an enemy's health from a safe distance before an all-in fight.
A short exchange of damage between opposing laners.
Starting a fight by committing to an attack on the enemy team.
Any crowd control effect, like a stun, root, or snare, that stops a champion from moving.
Effects like stuns, roots, and slows that limit an enemy's actions.
A short-range instant teleport used to reposition.
